compiled_state.title: --json everywhere: structured output for every command, each backed by a JSON Schema
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
Make every rebar command emit structured JSON, and make every JSON output shape
authoritatively documented by a JSON Schema validated in tests across all three
interfaces (CLI, library, MCP). Scope below is the result of a full codebase audit.

## Motivation
The output surface is inconsistent: some commands emit JSON, others ad-hoc text/
TSV/id-list, with no machine-readable contract. A prototype exists (canonical
TicketState schema + cross-interface test + MCP outputSchema for show_ticket).
This epic generalizes it. The schema arm is tracked in the child story
fe3e-346e-e5eb-42db (Roll out JSON Schemas + typed MCP returns + 2 bug fixes).

## Output inventory (audit result)

### Already JSON — no --json work (schema only, via the story)
show, list, search (TicketState[]), deps, list-descendants, get-file-impact,
get-verify-commands, clarity-check, scratch get/set/clear. Behind a flag:
next-batch (--json), validate (--json), bridge-status (--format=json).

### Needs a structured/--json form ADDED (epic work; each then needs a schema)
Primary data outputs with NO JSON path today:
- list-epics (TSV) -> {p0_bugs[],epics[{id,alias,priority,title,children_count,blocking,blocked,blockers[]}]}; preserve exit 0/1/2
- ready (id-list default) -> JSON array of ids (or ticket summaries); note --format=llm NDJSON already exists
- summary (text line) -> [{ticket_id,status,title,blocking_summary}]
- check-ac (AC_CHECK: ...) -> {verdict,criteria_count,reason}
- quality-check (QUALITY: ...) -> {verdict,line_count,keyword_count,ac_items,file_impact,reason}
- fsck (line-tagged text) -> {issues:[{kind,ticket_id?,filename?,detail}],fixed[],issue_count}
- bridge-fsck (text; findings dict already exists) -> {orphaned[],duplicates[],stale[]}
- transition / reopen (UNBLOCKED: ...) -> {ticket_id,from,to,newly_unblocked[]}; keep exit 10
- claim (CLAIMED: ...) -> {ticket_id,status,assignee}; keep exit 10
- create (human line + bare id) -> {id,alias,title} (removes the `| tail -1` id-scrape convention)
- delete (Deleted + UNBLOCKED:) -> {ticket_id,deleted,newly_unblocked[]}

### Side-effect / exit-code only — NO --json needed
comment, edit, tag, untag, set-file-impact, set-verify-commands, link (non-dry-run),
unlink, exists (boolean by exit code), init (operator bootstrap, stderr only).

### Borderline (convert only for 100% coverage; low value)
compact, compact-all, fsck-recover, purge-bridge, revert, archive, link --dry-run,
resolve, format. `format` is a display helper and should likely stay text.

## Cross-cutting requirements
1. FLAG STANDARDIZATION: today there are three conventions — `--json` (next-batch,
   validate), `--format=json` (bridge-status), `--format=llm` (show/list/ready).
   Pick ONE canonical structured-output flag and apply it everywhere (alias the
   others for back-compat). Decide & document.
2. SCHEMA-COVERAGE GUARD (key DoD): a test that enumerates every command's
   structured output and asserts (a) it validates against a schema and (b) no
   structured output lacks a schema — coverage is enforced, not aspirational.
3. MCP read tools advertise outputSchema (typed returns) consistent with the
   canonical schemas (delegated to the story).
4. stderr/stdout discipline: keep human/progress/WARNING lines on stderr so JSON
   stdout stays clean (audit noted show/list bridge-alert warnings, next-batch
   conflict matrix, validate human output, list-epics "no epics" all already on stderr).

## Suggested child-task breakdown (to be filed under this epic)
- T1: schema rollout + typed MCP returns + 2 bug fixes  (== story fe3e-346e-e5eb-42db)
- T2: choose & apply the canonical structured-output flag (standardization + back-comat aliases)
- T3: add --json to the lifecycle/result commands (create/claim/transition/reopen/delete)
- T4: add --json to the read/report commands (list-epics/ready/summary/check-ac/quality-check/fsck/bridge-fsck)
- T5: schema-coverage guard test + docs of the per-command output contract

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Inventory above is validated and turned into child tasks (T1-T5)
- [ ] Canonical structured-output flag chosen, applied everywhere, back-compat aliases kept
- [ ] Every data-returning command offers structured JSON (the "Needs --json" list above)
- [ ] A JSON Schema exists under src/rebar/schemas/ for every distinct JSON output shape
- [ ] Coverage-guard test: every structured output validates against its schema AND no structured output lacks a schema
- [ ] MCP read tools advertise outputSchema matching the canonical schemas
- [ ] Docs describe/link the per-command output contract (README/CLAUDE/docs)

comments.body:
VALIDATION (independent subagent, read-only). The output inventory above is
ACCURATE — all 42 bash dispatcher case arms verified, format classifications
CONFIRMED, and the flag-name inconsistency confirmed exactly: `--json`
(next-batch, validate) vs `--format=json` (bridge-status) vs `--format=llm`
(show/list/ready). Corrections/additions:

1. OMISSION — `reconcile` is a real CLI command and was NOT in the inventory.
   It is intercepted in the Python entry point (cli.py:34-35) and routed to
   `python -m rebar_reconciler` (NOT in the bash dispatcher). Its stdout is human
   text (format_report; "OK: applied N of M mutations"; "OK: no-op") and it also
   writes a JSON file under bridge_state/. NOTE: reconcile is the JIRA reconciler
   — explicitly OUT of scope for the original non-Jira audit. Decide whether the
   "--json everywhere" epic covers it (recommend: yes, add `--json` to surface the
   report it already builds, but track under Jira-bridge work).

2. Nuances (classification unchanged, wording sharpened):
   - `--format=llm` on `show` with a SINGLE id emits one JSON object (NDJSON only
     for multi-id show, and for list/ready --format=llm).
   - next-batch default stdout is tag-prefixed tab lines (EPIC:\t..., TASK:\t...,
     SKIPPED_*:\t...), not a uniform-column TSV; conflict matrix is on stderr.
   - list-epics exit codes: exit 1 = "No open epics found." (none exist); exit 2 =
     "All N open epics are blocked." (distinct case). Both stderr; JSON variant must
     preserve both exit codes/semantics.

3. stderr/stdout discipline CONFIRMED clean for adding JSON: validate human output,
   next-batch conflict matrix, show/list bridge-alert WARNINGs, and list-epics
   empty/blocked messages all already go to stderr — JSON on stdout won't collide.

See the child story's validation comment for the shapes/consolidation/typing
corrections (esp. the two-reducer show-vs-list divergence).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: FLAG DECISION (T2), maintainer-approved: canonical structured-output flag is `--output VALUE` with short alias `-o VALUE` (values: json|llm|text). Both legacy `--json` AND `--format=` are REMOVED (no back-compat aliases) — pre-1.0 breaking change. Rationale: OSS survey shows the selector convention dominates ~4:1 for multi-format tools, `-o/--output json` is the single most common spelling (kubectl/aws/az/helm), and `-o` is the expected short alias when the flag is named --output. rebar emits 2 machine formats (json+llm) so it's squarely selector-camp. Value syntax accepted: `-o json`, `--output json`, `--output=json`.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Inventory turned into child tasks (AC#1): T1=story fe3e-346e-e5eb-42db (schemas+typed MCP+2 bugfixes); T2=43b1-ff14-76ca-4c2f (flag standardize+centralize); T3=faa8-1361-0718-47a7 (--output on lifecycle cmds); T4=42a0-25c8-7d97-4764 (--output on report cmds); T5=a566-4a60-bad1-42d1 (coverage guard+docs). Execution order: T2 (foundational, centralized) -> T1/T4 (shapes+schemas) -> T3 -> T5.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: EPIC COMPLETE. All 5 stories closed across 6 commits (8ae4872 T2, 931b35c release-doc+probe, 45c4ae4 T1, b03d5a7 T3, ffe34ca T4, 0d0f859 T5). Canonical --output/-o flag everywhere (--json/--format removed); 21 JSON schemas (shared common.$defs) with a referencing-backed validator; every data-returning command emits structured JSON; MCP read tools advertise outputSchema; coverage guard enforces no structured output lacks a schema; docs/output-schemas.md documents the contract + source-of-truth. All format logic centralized in one Python module (ticket_output.py) per the no-duplicate-bash/Python principle. Full suite 1300 passed, 2 xfailed; live probe 141 passed. NOTE: deviation from the original 'back-compat aliases kept' AC — maintainer chose to DROP --json/--format entirely (pre-1.0 breaking change), which the survey + convention research supported.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
